data.title: 16 suspects arrested in South Korea for $28M hacking of crypto and bank accounts, including targeting K-pop star Jungkook and Korean investors.
data.description: Authorities in Seoul have apprehended a total of 16 individuals in connection with a large-scale theft amounting to $28 million from both cryptocurrency and bank accounts. The suspects specifically targeted prominent South Korean pop star Jungkook from the band BTS, as well as several affluent investors in the country.

data.title: Parisians celebrate Chinese Qixi Festival at Darry Ring store; couples embrace tradition with red-string ritual.
data.description: On August 29, in Paris, a large gathering of Parisians convened outside the Darry Ring flagship store at the Louvre to commemorate the Chinese Qixi Festival. This festival, known as the "Chinese Valentine’s Day," is steeped in the tale of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl. Celebrants flocked to Darry Ring's store to engage in the brand’s red-string ritual, a symbolic gesture connected to love in Chinese tradition. Among them, an international couple shared their experience.

data.title: Dell COO states no major impact from tariffs, company's second-quarter earnings call mentions deflationary input costs. Dell stock weak.
data.description: Dell Technologies Inc. (NYSE: DELL) is addressing trade and tariff uncertainties confidently, with COO Jeff Clarke assuring that the company anticipates minimal impact moving forward and has no intentions to raise prices for customers. Despite this, DELL stock is displaying notable weakness currently. During the second-quarter earnings call, Clarke stated that the company's full-year guidance incorporates all known tariff implications and they maintain a positive outlook on pricing. Dell witnessed deflationary input costs in the second quarter, further supporting their position. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: UTStarcom announces unaudited financial results for the first half of 2025, revealing business performance until June 30.
data.description: UTStarcom, a leading global telecommunications infrastructure provider, based in Hangzhou, China, recently released its unaudited financial report and business update for the first half of 2025. The report covers the period ending June 30, 2025. The company, listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol UTSI, shared the important financial figures for this period, showcasing its performance in the industry.

data.title: Youlife Group Inc. forms joint venture with Beijing Galbot Co., Ltd. to advance vocational education in China. #YOUL #partnership
data.description: On August 29, 2025 in Beijing, Youlife Group Inc. (Nasdaq: YOUL), a prominent blue-collar lifetime service provider in China, revealed the signing of a joint venture deal between its subsidiary and Beijing Galbot Co., Ltd. This strategic agreement is aimed at fostering collaboration and enhancing business operations within the two entities.
